---
title: "Euclid × SPT-3G CMB lensing"
subtitle: "The first Euclid (TR1) × SPT-3G κ cross-correlation"
occasion: SPT Summer Collaboration Meeting
description: First Euclid TR1 × SPT-3G CMB-lensing cross-correlation — a robust detection at a sub-unity amplitude
draft: true
author:
    name: Cail Daley
    email: cailmdaley@gmail.com
date: 2026-06-29
date-format: "MMMM YYYY"

output-file: index.html

format:
    revealjs:
        width:  1920
        height: 1080
        theme: [default, custom.scss]
        slide-number: true
        fig-align: center
        include-after-body: assets/no_footer_on_titleslide.html
        footer: Cail Daley | Euclid × SPT-3G CMB lensing | SPT Summer Collaboration | June 2026
        history: false
        hash : true
        pdf-separate-fragments : true
        pdf-max-pages-per-slide	 : 1
        template-partials:
            - title-slide.html
        center-title-slide: false
image: images/spt26_footprint.png

html-math-method:
    method: mathjax
    url: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-svg-full.js
include-in-header: assets/mathjax.html
---

## The bottom line, up front {.center}

We measure the **first Euclid (TR1) × SPT-3G CMB-lensing cross-correlation** —
galaxy clustering and weak-lensing shear against the SPT-3G winter GMV $\kappa$.

::: {.fragment}
The cross is **detected at high significance** (S/N up to ~12 per tomographic bin).
:::

::: {.fragment}
The single-amplitude fit sits **below** an untuned Planck-18 prediction:
$$\hat A = 0.63 \pm 0.05 \quad (\text{PTE } 0.73).$$
:::

::: {.fragment}
That sub-unity amplitude is **ℓ-flat and robust** — and it is the honest **open
question** I want to put to the collaboration, not a number to explain away.
:::

## Why cross-correlate CMB lensing with Euclid?

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
- CMB lensing $\kappa$ is a **clean, high-redshift mass map** — no galaxy bias, a
  well-understood kernel peaking at $z \sim 2$.
- Crossing $\kappa$ with Euclid **galaxy density** and **shear** pins the
  **growth of structure** and the **galaxy–matter connection** across $0 < z < 2$.
- The cross is a **self-calibration handle**: it is sensitive to shear
  multiplicative bias $m$ and the source $n(z)$ largely independently of the
  Euclid auto-spectra.
- SPT-3G + Euclid open a **new, deep southern overlap** at high lensing S/N.
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
- The cross-spectrum is also **immune to additive shear systematics and to galaxy
  shot noise** — they don't correlate with the CMB.
- For SPT: a Euclid cross is an **external, independent check** on the winter-field
  lensing reconstruction.
:::
::::::::::::::

## Euclid TR1: the new data going into the cross

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
- **TR1** ≈ the first ~**30% of the DR1 area**, used to validate methods ahead of
  DR1.
- Two tracers enter the cross:
  - **Galaxy clustering** $\delta_g$ — photometric sample, **6 tomographic bins**.
  - **Weak-lensing shear** $\gamma$ — **6 tomographic bins**.
- Spectra are measured with **NaMaster** pseudo-$C_\ell$ bandpowers on the joint
  Euclid × SPT mask.
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
- The data vector is a **5×2pt-style** set of auto- and cross-spectra; the **spine
  for this talk is the two CMB-lensing crosses**, $\delta_g\times\kappa$ and
  $\gamma\times\kappa$.
- Amplitudes are quoted against an **untuned Planck-18** fiducial — no tuning of
  $n(z)$, $m$, or IA. So $\hat A = 1$ would mean "Planck-18 as-is."
:::
::::::::::::::

## Where the cross lives: Euclid TR1 × SPT-3G in the south

::: {.r-stack}
![](images/spt26_footprint_1_euclid.png){.fragment .fade-out fragment-index=1 width="80%"}
![](images/spt26_footprint_2_spt.png){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=1 width="80%"}
![](images/spt26_footprint_3_act.png){.fragment .fade-in fragment-index=2 width="80%"}
:::

[Euclid grows **TR1 → DR1**; **+ SPT-3G main** lights the **149 deg²** patch the cross
lives on; **+ ACT DR6** adds a second κ survey (triple overlap 122 deg²).]{.credit style="font-size:0.55em"}

## Both CMB-lensing crosses are detected and track Planck-18

![](images/spt26_cross_spectra.png){width="92%"}

[$\gamma\times\kappa$ (top) and $\delta_g\times\kappa$ (bottom), six tomographic bins
each. **Per-bin S/N reaches ~12** at high redshift (≈6–12 across bins); per-bin
$\hat A$ sits **below unity** across the board.]{.credit style="font-size:0.6em"}

## A robust detection — at a sub-unity, ℓ-flat amplitude

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
![](images/spt26_amplitude_by_ell.png){width="100%"}
:::
::: {.column width="42%"}
- Headline: $\hat A = 0.63 \pm 0.05$, **PTE 0.73** (12 crosses, full Gaussian cov).
- **ℓ-flat at ~0.65** across $\ell \in [150, 3000]$ — *not* a high-ℓ or low-ℓ
  feature.
- $\ell < 150$ is **covariance-unstable** (cond $8.4\times10^5$) — consistent with
  *both* 0.63 and unity; we don't read a story into it.
- A **full Gaussian covariance only deepens** the deficit vs Knox-diagonal
  (orange): the misfit was Knox **over-tightening**, not the model fighting the
  data (cross-only PTE $1.9\times10^{-6}\to0.73$).
:::
::::::::::::::

## So what is an ℓ-independent ~0.65 deficit consistent with? {.center}

::: {.fragment}
**Favoured** (flat in ℓ, multiplicative):
a **normalization** offset — source $n(z)$ mean-$z$/tail, shear multiplicative bias
$m$, intrinsic alignments — **or a genuinely low amplitude**.
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Disfavoured** by the *wrong* ℓ-signature (these are not flat):
baryonic feedback, nonlinear / scale-dependent bias, a high-ℓ systematic.
:::

::: {.fragment}
This is where I'd value the collaboration's read — *the deficit is the result, and
its interpretation is open.*
:::

## The crosses are robust to the SPT lensing estimator

![](images/spt26_estimator_comparison.png){width="80%"}

[Swapping the GMV baseline for the **bias + profile-hardened** reconstruction moves
every bandpower by **<1σ** (RMS 0.28σ), at no S/N cost. This **bounds the tSZ/CIB
hardening correction at ≲0.9σ** — estimator-robust, but *not yet* a direct
foreground-bias measurement (the Websky/Agora-with-HOD recovery is reserved).]{.credit style="font-size:0.55em"}

## The one honest internal tension: a clustering–shear split

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
- The combined $\hat A$ is **ℓ-flat** — but that is an aggregate over a **~1.6σ
  split between the two cross families**:
  - $\delta_g \times \kappa$ (clustering): $\hat A = 0.72 \pm 0.06$
  - $\gamma \times \kappa$ (shear): $\hat A = 0.59 \pm 0.06$
- **ℓ-flat and probe-consistent are different axes.** A single "ℓ-independent
  normalization" must not silently absorb this offset.
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
- The split is visible per-bin in the detection figure (clustering crosses sit
  higher than shear crosses).
- If real, it points at a **tracer-specific** normalization ($m$ or shear $n(z)$
  vs galaxy $n(z)$/bias) rather than a global low amplitude.
:::
::::::::::::::

## Two CMB-lensing surveys, one consistent cross

![](images/spt26_cross_survey.png){width="92%"}

[The same Euclid tracers crossed with **SPT-3G GMV** (red) and **ACT DR6** (teal)
give **consistent** cross-spectra bin-by-bin — the detection is not specific to one
reconstruction.]{.credit style="font-size:0.6em"}

## Galaxy clustering is consistent — but doesn't yet break A–b

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
![](images/spt26_dd_joint_fit.png){width="100%"}
:::
::: {.column width="42%"}
- Adding $\delta_g\delta_g$ to the joint fit **tightens galaxy bias ~2.5×** over
  $\delta_g\times\kappa$ alone.
- The 3-probe fit is **consistent** with a single sub-unity amplitude:
  $\hat A = 0.55 \pm 0.07$, $\chi^2 = 9.4/6$, **PTE 0.15**.
- But it **does not yet break the amplitude–bias degeneracy**, and the per-bin
  panel flags real structure: **bin 4 at −2.4σ**, a coherent **+3.2σ tilt at
  ℓ≈130–390**, and the ℓ≈84.5 SPT spike.
:::
::::::::::::::

## GLASS mocks: the clustering high-ℓ rise is gridding aliasing

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
![](images/spt26_glass_recovery.png){width="100%"}
:::
::: {.column width="40%"}
- Known-truth **GLASS** mocks (south, 100 realizations): recovery tracks input
  through the **bulk** of the range.
- The **rise above input at high ℓ** is **NGP gridding aliasing** — proven by a
  regrid fixed-ℓ test (excess created at the analysis grid, vanishes at nside 2048),
  consistent with Wolz 2024 App. B.
- Sets an **empirical scale cut $\ell \lesssim 865$** (≤5% aliasing). Mechanism is
  nailed; there is **no closed-form factor** — the cut is set empirically.
:::
::::::::::::::

## What is *not* yet under control — stated plainly {.center}

::: {.fragment}
**Shear auto-spectrum ($\gamma\gamma$):** the on-disk product is **broken** —
negative $C_\ell$, $E\approx B$, a spin-2×spin-2 estimator pathology at low $f_{\rm
sky}$. **No $\hat A_{\gamma\gamma}$ yet**; the shear-cal cross-check is pending the fix.
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Systematics:** quantified for **galactic extinction only** (~1.5σ aggregate, and
stale). Stellar density, exposures, zodiacal, N/S asymmetry are **un-redone** on this
catalog. **One number does not close systematics.**
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Blinding:** cross-correlations are **out of SGS blinding scope by construction**;
we roll a **standalone loose self-blind** (a single secret amplitude factor) on the
talk timescale.
:::

## Summary & outlook {.center}

- **First Euclid (TR1) × SPT-3G CMB-lensing cross-correlation** — detected at high
  S/N across 12 tomographic crosses.
- Single-amplitude fit: $\hat A = 0.63 \pm 0.05$ (PTE 0.73), **ℓ-flat at ~0.65**,
  **covariance- and estimator-robust**.
- The **sub-unity, ℓ-flat amplitude is the open question**: normalization
  ($n(z)$ / $m$ / IA) or a genuinely low amplitude — *not* baryons or high-ℓ
  systematics.

::: {.fragment}
**Next:** fix $\gamma\gamma$ → $\hat A_{\gamma\gamma}$ as a shear-cal discriminant ·
joint per-bin $\{A, b_i\}$ fit · mock-based covariance · close systematics · DR1.
:::

## Thanks {.center}

Euclid CMBX SWG — **Margherita Lembo, Louis Legrand, Giulio Fabbian** and the
3×2pt+κ team — and the **SPT-3G lensing** effort whose winter GMV $\kappa$ maps make
this cross possible.

[Built on Euclid TR1 (RR2) × SPT-3G winter GMV. Amplitudes vs untuned Planck-18; full
data-fiducial Gaussian covariance.]{.credit style="font-size:0.6em"}
